step_by_step_thinking:
Extraction of disto-angular impaction of mandibular 3rd molar can lead to excessive hemorrhage (\boxed{C}). Given the complexities involved with disto-angular impactions, particularly close proximity to nerves and delicate anatomy, managing hemostasis becomes critical. Although slippage in the lingual pouch could theoretically happen, it is not specifically highlighted as a primary complication among the given articles. Damage to the rami of the mandible is too broad and not directly linked to this type of impaction. Lastly, while dry sockets do pose a risk, they are secondary to inadequate management rather than inherent to the process itself.
\boxed{C} Based on the detailed review of the literature, the correct option aligns with the recognized challenges posed by disto-angular impactions regarding hemostasis and the intricate nature of the surgical approach needed. Thus,
